explanation: The loop should use hasNext to test for another token, next to read it, and close the Scanner after all input has been read.

stem: Which code segment correctly creates a File object for "students.txt" and then creates a Scanner to read from that file?

options.label: A
options.text:
File f = new File("students.txt");
Scanner scan = new Scanner(f);

options.label: B
options.text:
File f = "students.txt";
Scanner scan = new Scanner(f);

options.label: C
options.text:
File f = new File("students.txt");
Scanner scan = Scanner(f);

options.label: D
options.text:
File f = new File();
Scanner scan = new Scanner("students.txt");

explanation: A File is constructed with new File("students.txt"), and the Scanner is constructed with new Scanner(f).

options.label: A
options.text: public static void readData() throws IOException

explanation: Constructing a Scanner with a File can throw an IOException, so the method header should include throws IOException.

options.label: A
options.text: The Scanner is closed inside the loop, before all input is read.

options.label: B
options.text: The File object must be closed instead of the Scanner.

options.label: C
options.text: The call to scan.next() should be before the while loop.

options.label: D
options.text: The ArrayList must be constructed after the loop.

explanation: The Scanner should remain open while the loop is reading from the file. The close call belongs after the loop.
The next method reads one token at a time. The four tokens are red, blue, green, and yellow.
Each call to nextLine reads an entire line, so the ArrayList receives two strings.

stem: Each line of a file has the form word:syn1,syn2,syn3. Which code correctly stores the part before the colon in key and the comma-separated synonyms in an array named vals?

options.label: A
B
C
D

explanation: The line is first split at the colon. The first piece is the word, and the second piece is split at commas to get the synonyms.
The loop reads the three integers 4, 7, and 2, and their sum is 13.

stem: A file contains one book title per line, and some titles contain spaces. Which code best reads each title as one String into titles?
explanation: Because a title may contain spaces, nextLine is needed to read the whole line as one String. The Scanner is closed after reading.

stem:
A file items.txt contains this text.

pencil,12
pen,8

Inside a loop, line stores one line from the file. Which code correctly prints the item name from that line?
explanation: The line uses a comma as the separator, and the item name appears before the comma, at index 0.

options.label: A
options.text: It reads all tokens from words.txt into list.

options.label: B
options.text: It reads only the first token because close is inside the loop.

options.label: C
options.text: It will not compile because throws IOException is missing.

options.label: D
options.text: It creates a Scanner, but it never creates a File.

explanation: The Scanner is constructed from a File, the loop reads all tokens, and close is correctly placed after the loop.
The loop continues while there is input remaining, nextLine reads each complete line, and close is after the loop.

options.label: A
options.text: public static void readScores() throws IOException

options.label: B
options.text: public static void readScores() throws Scanner

options.label: C
options.text: public static File readScores() throws Scanner

explanation: A method that constructs a Scanner from a File should include throws IOException in its header.
The tokens are stored in order: cat at index 0, dog at index 1, fish at index 2, and bird at index 3.
Splitting blue:sky,ocean at the colon produces parts[0] as blue and parts[1] as sky,ocean.

stem: A file contains one integer score per line. Which code correctly counts how many scores are greater than 90?

options.label: A
C
D

explanation: The loop checks whether another value exists, reads it with nextInt, and increments count only when the value is greater than 90.

stem: Which code segment correctly opens the file words.txt without first storing the File object in a variable?
explanation: The File object may be created directly as the argument to the Scanner constructor.
Each token is read by next and followed by an exclamation point in result.

stem:
A file has lines in this form:

name,grade

Which code correctly reads each line and adds only the name to an ArrayList<String> names?

options.label: A
B
C
D

explanation: Each line is read, split at the comma, and the name before the comma is added to the ArrayList.

stem: Which code segment contains a compile-time error?

options.label: A
options.text:
File f = new File("words.txt");
Scanner scan = new Scanner(f);

options.label: C
options.text:
File f = "words.txt";
Scanner scan = new Scanner(f);

options.label: D

explanation: A String literal cannot be assigned directly to a File variable. A File object must be constructed with new File(...).
The correct code reads each token with next, adds it to vals, closes the Scanner after the loop, and returns the list.
Splitting the line at commas produces three strings: rice, beans, and corn.

options.label: D
options.text: Construct the ArrayList after the loop.

explanation: next reads a single token, so it stops at spaces. nextLine reads the entire phrase line.

stem: Which statement best explains why scan.close() should usually appear after a file-reading loop?

options.label: A
options.text: Closing the Scanner before or during the loop can prevent the rest of the file from being read.

options.label: B
options.text: Closing the Scanner changes all remaining file tokens into lines.

options.label: C
options.text: Closing the Scanner is required before each call to next.

options.label: D
options.text: Closing the Scanner creates the File object.

explanation: The Scanner must remain open while input is being read. It should be closed after the reading loop is complete.
